name: Aura - Single Webhook Per-ASIN Pipeline v4 Crawbase

parameters.jsCode:
const d = $input.item.json; const config = $('Setup Global Config').first().json; let categoryRules = {}; try { categoryRules = $('Lookup Category Rules').first().json || {}; } catch (e) { categoryRules = {}; } const originalAsins = $('Parse ASINs').first().json.asins || []; const specific_claims = d['Specific Claims'] || categoryRules['Specific Claims'] || ''; const specific_rules = d['Specific Rules'] || categoryRules['Specific Rules'] || ''; const specific_specs = d['Specific Specs'] || categoryRules['Specific Specs'] || ''; const prompt = `You write buyer-focused product reviews for Aura Home Office, an independent home office buying guide for American shoppers. OUTPUT: One valid raw JSON object. First char {. Last char }. No markdown syntax anywhere. Do not use **, ##, backticks, or markdown bullets inside JSON strings. No text before or after. No trailing commas. All string values must be valid JSON strings. Escape any double quote that appears inside a string. In custom_tabs content strings, use only these simple HTML tags:

,

    ,
  • , , ,
    . Do not add HTML attributes, classes, styles, onclicks, tables, links, or unescaped quotation marks inside HTML strings. ## CLAIM RULES ${specific_claims} Never use hype: best seller, top rated, premium, ultimate, game-changer, guaranteed, revolutionize. Never fake personal use: I used, I tested, after two weeks, in my hands, in my experience. Never use test, tested, hands-on, lab-tested, or personally tested unless explicit first-party testing data is provided. Never disclose AI or apologize for limits. Write like a strict editor summarizing product evidence. ## REVIEW DATA RULES Use ONLY listing specs, product details, and Top Reviews when Top Reviews is non-empty. DO NOT hallucinate, infer, or guess specifications. If a requested spec is missing from the product data, explicitly write "Not specified". If Top Reviews has useful patterns: use "Owner feedback points to..." / "Several reviewers mention..." If Top Reviews is empty: do not mention reviews, owner feedback, missing reviews, no reviews, unverified comfort claims, or absent buyer feedback. Base the review only on listing specs, product details, and concrete specs buyers should verify. If product data is sparse, say what buyers should verify instead of inventing specs. === CATEGORY SPECIFIC FOCUS === ${specific_rules} ## SEO AND RETENTION RULES 1. custom_title must contain the exact product type keyword. Max 150 chars. 2. short_review must mention product type in the first sentence. 3. Explain who should buy it and who should skip it explicitly. 4. Make every point concrete enough for comparison against other ASINs. 5. Avoid generic AI phrasing: moreover, furthermore, in conclusion, important to note, when it comes to, delving into, seamless. 6. Keep best_for, skip_if, decision_note, and setup_context to one short sentence each. 7. Create a short_product_name that is 2-6 words. Do not copy the full Amazon title. 8. Create a product_label in Title Case, e.g. Best Overall, Best Budget, Best For Dual Monitors, Best For Small Desks. 9. Pros and cons must be short bullet fragments under 10 words each. 10. Do not invent price or value claims. If price data is missing, write "Check current price" or focus on specs. 11. Write for conversion without hype: identify the buyer's real job-to-be-done, the purchase objection, and the reason this product is worth clicking over similar options. 12. Every comparison_summary field must help the macro article sell: best_for should name a buyer situation, skip_if should remove a bad-fit buyer, main_strength should be the click reason, and main_weakness should be the honest objection. 13. decision_note must be decisive and useful: "Buy this if...", "Compare it if...", or "Skip it if...". Do not write a vague summary. 14. evidence_points should include proof-like details from specs or owner feedback when provided, not marketing adjectives. ## PRODUCT DATA ASIN: ${d.asin} Title: ${d.title} Brand: ${d.brand} Rating: ${d.rating}/5 Description: ${d.description} Features: ${d.features} Specifications: ${d.specifications} Top Reviews: ${d.top_reviews} ## KEY SPECS TO EXTRACT ${specific_specs} ## AURA SCORE RULES Return score as a number from 0 to 10 with one decimal using Aura's public rating scale: - 9.0-10.0 Gold Standard: very strong on the available evidence and a leading option for the right workspace and budget. - 7.5-8.9 Recommended: strong performer with useful features and clear fit for many home offices. - 6.0-7.4 Functional: meets expectations well, though some trade-offs in materials or features exist. - 4.0-5.9 Average: functional but often outclassed by better options in the same price range. - 0-3.9 Poor: significant drawbacks or durability concerns. Not recommended for daily work. Aura score is a shortlist score, not a harsh catalog-quality score. Use this formula: - If Amazon rating is available: score = Amazon rating + 5.0. - Round to one decimal. - Cap score at 9.9. - Example: 4.5/5 becomes 9.5/10. 4.3/5 becomes 9.3/10. 5.0/5 becomes 9.9/10. - If Amazon rating is missing but the product data is usable, use a reasonable shortlist score between 8.1 and 8.8 based on evidence completeness, setup fit, and trade-offs. - Do not give every usable product the same fallback score. Vary scores slightly so the shortlist reflects meaningful comparison judgment. Missing specs, sparse listing data, or missing Top Reviews should affect claim_confidence, missing_specs, and score_rationale, not automatically drag the score below 8.1 for a usable shortlisted product. ## OUTPUT FORMAT { "short_product_name": "2-6 words. Brand + model or clean product name. No keyword stuffing.", "product_label": "Title Case buying label, e.g. Best Overall or Best For Dual Monitors.", "custom_title": "Max 150 chars. Exact product type + main use case + key differentiator. No hype.", "short_review": "2-3 sentences. What it is, who it fits, one honest trade-off.", "evidence_basis": "Research-based using listing specs and product details. Mention buyer feedback only when Top Reviews is non-empty.", "source_basis": "One of: listing specs + owner feedback, listing specs only, sparse listing data.", "claim_confidence": "high, medium, or low based on data completeness.", "score": "Number 0-10 with one decimal, e.g. 7.8 or 8.4. Must follow the Aura Score Rules.", "score_label": "Gold Standard, Recommended, Functional, Average, or Poor.", "score_rationale": "One sentence explaining why this score fits the available evidence.", "missing_specs": ["Important missing specs buyers should verify. Use [] if none."], "selling_points": ["5 items exactly. Concrete benefit with home office context."], "pros": ["5-6 items. Under 10 words each. Concrete advantage from product data."], "cons": ["2-3 items. Under 10 words each. Honest limitation from specs."], "comparison_summary": { "comparison_angle": "Short buying label tied to buyer intent, e.g. Best for dual monitors, Best renter-friendly pick, or Best for heavy cable bundles.", "best_for": "1 short sentence naming the buyer situation, workspace constraint, or setup where this product is easiest to recommend.", "skip_if": "1 short sentence naming the buyer who should avoid it because of fit, mounting, capacity, comfort, or missing specs.", "main_strength": "The strongest click reason from specs or Top Reviews when provided. Make it concrete enough to compare.", "main_weakness": "The main purchase objection or buying risk a cautious shopper would notice.", "key_specs": ["3-5 concise specs that affect buying decisions. Use 'Not specified' if missing."], "evidence_points": ["3 proof-like bullets from specs or Top Reviews when provided. Avoid adjectives without evidence."], "decision_note": "One decisive sentence that starts with Buy, Compare, or Skip and explains why.", "setup_context": "The specific home-office setup where this product makes the most sense." }, "custom_tabs": [ {"title": "Why It Stands Out", "content": "

    Main reason to consider it: 2-3 sentences.

    What that means daily: How key feature helps in WFH setup.

    ", "icon": "bi-stars"}, {"title": "Before You Buy", "content": "

    Check before buying: Intro.

    • Concern 1: Why it matters.
    • Concern 2: What to verify.
    • Concern 3: What to confirm.
    ", "icon": "bi-chat-square-text"}, {"title": "Key Specifications", "content": "

    Core specs:

    • Spec 1
    • Spec 2

    Verify before ordering: fit, weight, dimensions.

    ", "icon": "bi-list-check"}, {"title": "Best Setup & Trade-Offs", "content": "

    Best setup: Realistic workspace.

    Best for: Specific buyer type.

    Skip it if: Who should look elsewhere.

    Bottom line: Buy, skip, or compare.

    ", "icon": "bi-exclamation-triangle"} ] } VERIFY: Valid JSON with properly escaped quotes in all strings. Include short_product_name, product_label, source_basis, claim_confidence, score, score_label, score_rationale, and missing_specs. 5 selling_points, 5-6 pros, 2-3 cons, 4 tabs, comparison_summary complete. No fake price claims. No fake testing. No markdown. Use "Not specified" for missing specs.`; return { json: { ...config, ...d, asins: originalAsins, specific_claims, specific_rules, specific_specs, micro_prompt: prompt } };

value.wp_post_id: ={{ $('Build WP Category Update').first().json.wp_post_id || $('WP: Publish Article').first().json.post_id || $('WP: Publish Article').first().json.id }}
value.notes: ={{ ['Completed ' + new Date().toISOString(), 'job_group_id=' + ($('Build Article Payload').first().json.job_group_id || $('Normalize Input').first().json.job_group_id || ''), 'macro_model=' + ($('Build Article Payload').first().json.ai_model || ''), 'macro_tokens=' + (($('Build Article Payload').first().json.ai_input_tokens || 0) + '/' + ($('Build Article Payload').first().json.ai_output_tokens || 0)), 'macro_cost_est=$' + ($('Build Article Payload').first().json.ai_cost_estimate || 0), $('Normalize Input').first().json.category_inferred ? 'auto_category=' + $('Build Macro Prompt').first().json.category : '', 'asin_source=' + ($('Parse ASINs').first().json.asins_source || 'unknown'), 'category_set_in_create_post', $('Build Article Payload').first().json.meta?._aura_article_warnings || ''].filter(Boolean).join(' | ') }}

parameters.jsCode: return $input.all().filter(item => !(item.json || {})._fatal_error);

value.notes: ={{ ['Started ' + new Date().toISOString(), 'job_group_id=' + ($('Normalize Input').first().json.job_group_id || ''), $('Normalize Input').first().json.category_inferred ? 'auto_category=' + $('Normalize Input').first().json.category : '', $('Normalize Input').first().json.notes || ''].filter(Boolean).join(' | ') }}
